reviews.author: Jeff Linux-f2fs-devel
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the patch's removal of simple_nosetlease() function is unexpected because NFS4 provides a setlease operation, and expressed confusion about why lstat() returns -EINVAL in this case.
reviews.raw_body:
My apologies. I missed seeing this last week.

That's a very simple reproducer! That's very strange behavior,
especially since NFS4 does provide a setlease operation:

const struct file_operations nfs4_file_operations = {
	[...]
	.setlease       = nfs4_setlease,
	[...]
};

I'm not sure why this would cause lstat() to return -EINVAL. What's
happening on the wire when this occurs?

I'll plan to take a look here soon either way.
-- 
Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>


_______________________________________________
Linux-f2fs-devel mailing list
Linux-f2fs-devel@lists.sourceforge.net
https://lists.sourceforge.net/lists/listinfo/linux-f2fs-devel

reviews.author: Mike Snitzer
reviews.summary: reviewer expressed uncertainty about the patch's behavior, specifically why lstat() returns -EINVAL when setlease is called, and plans to investigate further
reviews.raw_body:
I'll have to revisit myself, been a bit.

Will let you know.

Thanks,
Mike

______________________________________________________
Linux MTD discussion mailing list
http://lists.infradead.org/mailman/listinfo/linux-mtd/

2026-03-04.patch_summary: This patch removes the simple_nosetlease() function from the Linux kernel's file system code, which was causing unexpected behavior when attempting to set a lease on an NFS4 file.
